annotations.label: Projective module (informal)

anchor.snippet: the class of projective modules enlarges the class of free modules

annotations.note: Module.Projective R P is the class for an R-module being projective.

annotations.label: Free implies projective; converse fails

anchor.snippet: Every free module is a projective module, but the converse fails to hold over some rings

annotations.note: Projective.of_free shows free modules are projective; the 'converse fails' assertion is not itself a formal statement.

annotations.label: Projective is free over a PID (Quillen–Suslin)

anchor.snippet: every projective module is a free module if the ring is a principal ideal domain such as the integers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves submodules of free modules over a PID are free, but not the full 'every projective is free over a PID' as one declaration.

annotations.label: Lifting property characterization

anchor.snippet: a module P is projective if and only if for every surjective module homomorphism

annotations.note: projective_lifting_property and Projective.of_lifting_property give both directions of the lifting characterization.

annotations.label: Split-exact sequence characterization

anchor.snippet: A module P is projective if and only if every short exact sequence of modules

annotations.note: iff_split_of_projective plus Projective.of_split express that a surjection onto P splits iff P is projective.

annotations.label: Direct summand of free module characterization

anchor.snippet: there is another module Q such that the direct sum of P and Q is a free module

annotations.note: Projective.iff_split states P is projective iff it is a direct summand (split) of a free module.

annotations.label: Exact functor characterization

anchor.snippet: the covariant functor Hom( P , -): R - Mod → Ab is an exact functor

annotations.note: An object is projective iff its preadditive coyoneda functor Hom(P,-) preserves epimorphisms (i.e. is exact).

annotations.label: Dual basis characterization

anchor.snippet: for every x in P , f i ( x ) is only nonzero for finitely many i

annotations.note: The very definition projective_def, a splitting of Finsupp.linearCombination, is the dual-basis formulation.

annotations.label: Direct sums/summands of projectives are projective

anchor.snippet: Direct sums and direct summands of projective modules are projective.

annotations.note: Projective.of_split handles summands and instances for P × Q and Π₀ handle (finite/dfinsupp) direct sums.

annotations.label: Re is projective for idempotent e

anchor.snippet: If e = e 2 is an idempotent in the ring R , then Re is a projective left module over R .

annotations.note: No declaration stating that the left ideal Re for an idempotent e is projective was found.

annotations.label: Ideals of a product ring are projective not free

anchor.snippet: are projective modules, since their direct sum (as R -modules) equals the free R -module R

annotations.note: The projective part follows from of_split (summand of free), but this specific product-ring example and its non-freeness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Implications among module properties

anchor.snippet: The left-to-right implications are true over any ring

annotations.note: Individual implications (free→projective, projective→flat) exist, but the full chain of module-property implications is not a single statement.

annotations.label: Free implies projective

anchor.snippet: Any free module is projective.

annotations.note: Projective.of_free is the instance that every free module is projective.

annotations.label: Converse over a field or skew field

anchor.snippet: if R is a field or skew field : any module is free in this case

annotations.note: Module.Free.of_divisionRing states every module over a division ring (field/skew field) is free.

annotations.label: Projective abelian groups are free

anchor.snippet: an abelian group is projective if and only if it is a free abelian group

annotations.note: PID infrastructure gives the result for finite-type ℤ-modules, but the general 'projective abelian group is free' iff is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Projective is free over a local ring

anchor.snippet: if the ring R is a local ring . This fact is the basis of the intuition

annotations.note: Mathlib proves finite flat (hence finite projective) modules over a local ring are free, but not Kaplansky's general non-finite case.

annotations.label: Idempotent ideals in a product ring are projective not free

anchor.snippet: Over a direct product of rings R × S where R and S are nonzero rings, both R × 0 and 0 × S are non-free projective modules.

annotations.note: Projectivity of such summands follows from of_split, but this R×0 / 0×S example and its non-freeness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-principal ideal over a Dedekind domain

anchor.snippet: Over a Dedekind domain a non- principal ideal is always a projective module that is not a free module.

annotations.note: No declaration stating non-principal ideals of a Dedekind domain are projective-but-not-free was found.

annotations.label: Natural module over a matrix ring

anchor.snippet: Over a matrix ring M n ( R ), the natural module R n is projective but is not free when n > 1.

annotations.note: No declaration about the natural module over a matrix ring being projective-not-free was found.

annotations.label: Modules over a semisimple ring

anchor.snippet: Over a semisimple ring , every module is projective, but a nonzero proper left (or right) ideal is not a free module.

annotations.note: projective_of_isSemisimpleRing shows every module over a semisimple ring is projective (the non-free-ideal remark is not formalized).

annotations.label: Projective implies flat

anchor.snippet: Every projective module is flat .

annotations.note: Flat.of_projective is the instance that projective modules are flat.

annotations.label: Q is flat but not projective

anchor.snippet: the abelian group Q is a Z -module that is flat, but not projective.

annotations.note: No declaration asserting ℚ is a flat-but-not-projective ℤ-module was found.

annotations.label: Finitely related flat implies projective

anchor.snippet: a finitely related flat module is projective.

annotations.note: Mathlib proves finitely presented flat modules are projective, a special case of the finitely related hypothesis.

annotations.label: Govorov–Lazard: flat = direct limit of f.g. free

anchor.snippet: a module M is flat if and only if it is a direct limit of finitely-generated free modules

annotations.note: No formalization of the Govorov–Lazard 'flat iff direct limit of f.g. free' characterization was found.

annotations.label: Raynaud–Gruson characterization of projectivity

anchor.snippet: a module M is projective if and only if it satisfies the following conditions

annotations.note: The Raynaud–Gruson conditions characterizing projectivity are not formalized.

annotations.label: Projectivity satisfies faithfully flat descent

anchor.snippet: the property of being projective satisfies faithfully flat descent

annotations.note: Faithfully flat descent of projectivity is not formalized; the file even lists Zariski-locality of projectivity as a TODO.

annotations.label: Left hereditary ring

anchor.snippet: a ring R for which every submodule of a projective left module is projective is called left hereditary

annotations.note: No notion of (left) hereditary ring was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quotients of projectives need not be projective

anchor.snippet: Quotients of projective modules also need not be projective, for example Z / n is a quotient of Z

annotations.note: This counterexample (ℤ/n a non-projective quotient of ℤ) is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Category of f.g. projectives is exact

anchor.snippet: The category of finitely generated projective modules over a ring is an exact category .

annotations.note: No exact-category structure on finitely generated projective modules was found.

annotations.label: Projective resolution

anchor.snippet: a projective resolution of M is an infinite exact sequence of modules

annotations.note: ProjectiveResolution is defined for objects in any category with zero morphisms, specializing to modules.

annotations.label: Every module has a projective resolution

anchor.snippet: Every module possesses a projective resolution.

annotations.note: ModuleCat.enoughProjectives gives enough projectives, yielding HasProjectiveResolutions for every module.

annotations.label: Koszul complex resolution

anchor.snippet: A classic example of a projective resolution is given by the Koszul complex of a regular sequence

annotations.note: The Koszul complex as a projective resolution is not formalized (only listed as a TODO for regular sequences).

annotations.label: Length of a finite resolution

anchor.snippet: The length of a finite resolution is the index n such that P n is nonzero

annotations.note: No standalone 'length of a finite projective resolution' definition was found.

annotations.label: Projective dimension

anchor.snippet: the minimal length among all finite projective resolutions of M is called its projective dimension

annotations.note: projectiveDimension is defined (via Ext-vanishing) in any abelian category, specializing to modules.

annotations.label: Modules of projective dimension zero

anchor.snippet: consider a module M such that pd( M ) = 0

annotations.note: projective_iff_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_one identifies projective dimension ≤ 0 with being projective.

annotations.label: Localization of a projective module is projective

anchor.snippet: The localization of a projective module is a projective module over the localized ring.

annotations.note: projective_of_isLocalizedModule shows a localized projective module is projective over the localized ring.

annotations.label: Projective over local ring is free

anchor.snippet: A projective module over a local ring is free.

annotations.note: Mathlib proves finite flat (hence finite projective) modules over a local ring are free, but not the general non-finite case.

annotations.label: Locally free = projective over Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: a finitely generated module over a commutative Noetherian ring is locally free if and only if it is projective.

annotations.note: freeLocus_eq_univ_iff states that for a finitely presented module, being free at every stalk is equivalent to being projective.

annotations.label: Locally free non-projective over a Boolean ring

anchor.snippet: there are examples of finitely generated modules over a non-Noetherian ring that are locally free and not projective.

annotations.note: No such locally-free-but-not-projective counterexample over a Boolean/non-Noetherian ring is formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalences for finitely presented modules

anchor.snippet: for finitely presented modules M over a commutative ring R

annotations.note: Some equivalences (locally free ⟺ projective, flat ⟺ projective for f.p.) are formalized, but not as a single bundled TFAE.

annotations.label: Constant rank over a Noetherian integral domain

anchor.snippet: if R is a Noetherian integral domain

annotations.note: Rank is shown locally constant for flat f.p. modules, but constancy over a Noetherian integral domain is not stated directly.

annotations.label: A is a direct factor of B

anchor.snippet: then A is a direct factor of B

annotations.note: Direct-summand/splitting infrastructure (of_split, iff_split_of_projective) exists, but this specific 'A is a direct factor of B' proposition is not isolated.

annotations.label: Rank at a prime ideal

anchor.snippet: The rank of P at a prime ideal

annotations.note: rankAtStalk p is defined as the rank of the localized module Mₚ over Rₚ.

annotations.label: Rank is locally constant

anchor.snippet: It is a locally constant function on X .

annotations.note: isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk shows rankAtStalk is locally constant for flat finitely presented modules (which includes finite projectives).

annotations.label: Constant rank over connected spectrum

anchor.snippet: if X is connected (that is if R has no other idempotents than 0 and 1), then P has constant rank.

annotations.note: Local constancy of rank is formalized, but the specialization 'constant rank when Spec R is connected' is not stated.

annotations.label: Serre–Swan theorem

anchor.section: Vector bundles and locally free modules
anchor.snippet: a finitely generated projective module over the space of smooth functions on a compact manifold is the space of smooth sections of a smooth vector bundle

annotations.note: The Serre–Swan theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector bundles are locally free

anchor.section: Vector bundles and locally free modules
anchor.snippet: Vector bundles are locally free .

annotations.note: No statement equating modules of sections of vector bundles with locally free modules was found.

annotations.label: Quillen–Suslin theorem

anchor.section: Projective modules over a polynomial ring
anchor.snippet: then every projective module over R is free

annotations.note: The Quillen–Suslin theorem (projective modules over polynomial rings are free) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Counterexample for induction on variables

anchor.section: Projective modules over a polynomial ring
anchor.snippet: A counterexample occurs with R equal to the local ring of the curve

annotations.note: This counterexample regarding induction on variables is not formalized.
